model.name: ADR.29 — OAuth 2.0 + OpenID Connect for Identification, Authentication, Authorization
model.documentation: Architectural decision to standardize identification/authentication/authorization across flexibility market participants using OAuth 2.0 as the authorization framework with OpenID Connect where authentication/identification is needed.

element.name: Flexibility Market Participants
element.documentation: Aggregators, DSOs, BRPs, FSPs and other ecosystem actors requiring secure cross-platform access.

element.name: Need for secure, standardized cross-platform access
element.documentation: Lack of standardized IAM leads to inconsistent enforcement, limited auditability, and increased risk.

element.name: Regulatory and market compliance
element.documentation: GDPR, grid codes, and EU cybersecurity/energy interoperability frameworks (e.g., NIS2, IEC 62325).

element.name: Least-privilege, delegated and fine-grained access control
element.documentation: Scopes, roles, claims; consistent model across human and machine clients.

element.name: Federated identity and SSO across participants
element.documentation: Support federated identity and shared trust framework; enables SSO.

element.name: Auditability and traceability of access
element.documentation: Support oversight, compliance, and forensic investigation.

element.name: Interoperability and ecosystem support
element.documentation: Standards-based approach with strong library/IdP support.

element.name: Secure token storage
element.documentation: No refresh tokens in localStorage; use secure cookies or mobile secure stores.

element.name: CSRF and nonce validation for front-end flows
High availability for Authorization Server / IdP
element.documentation: Replication/failover; SLA and monitoring.

element.name: Secure key storage
element.documentation: HSM or equivalent.

element.name: Monitoring, audit logging, rate-limiting and anomaly detection
Standardized identification, authentication and authorization across all clients
element.documentation: Human, machine, and system clients.

element.name: Centralized security and policy enforcement
element.documentation: Centralize scope-to-role-to-permission mapping and consistent enforcement.

element.name: Support token revocation and credential rotation
element.documentation: Respond to evolving security threats and incidents.

element.name: Support federated identity participation
element.documentation: Organizations may maintain their own IdPs while participating in shared trust.

element.name: Operational complexity risk
element.documentation: IdP management, key rotation, and operational overhead.

element.name: Authorization Server availability dependency risk
element.documentation: Increased dependency on centralized Authorization Server uptime.

element.name: Legacy migration/refactoring risk
element.documentation: Legacy systems may require refactoring to adopt OAuth2/OIDC.

element.name: Misconfiguration vulnerability risk
element.documentation: Configuration errors can introduce security vulnerabilities.

element.name: Identity and Access Management (IAM)
element.documentation: Capability to identify, authenticate, authorize and audit access across participants.

element.name: Adopt OAuth 2.0 + OpenID Connect
element.documentation: OAuth2 for delegated authorization; OIDC for identification/authentication where needed.

element.name: Operate centralized Authorization Server / IdP
element.documentation: Provide OIDC, token issuance, revocation, key management, and policy enforcement.

element.name: Authorization Policies (scopes/roles/claims mapping)
element.documentation: Central mapping for fine-grained permissions.

element.name: Cryptographic Keys and JWKS
element.documentation: Signing keys and published JWKS for token verification.

element.name: Security Monitoring and Audit
element.documentation: Monitoring, audit logging, anomaly detection and incident response support.

element.name: Market Participant
element.documentation: Role representing organizations participating in the flexibility market.

element.name: Access Operational Constraints Information
element.documentation: Plan/forecast/activate flexibility services using operational constraints and related information.

element.name: Operational Constraints Data
element.documentation: Sensitive operational data used for planning and activation.

element.name: Personal Data
element.documentation: Personal data governed by GDPR.

element.name: Secure and Auditable Data Access
element.documentation: Standardized secure, role-based, auditable access across platforms.

element.name: Market Rules and Data Access Agreement
element.documentation: Contractual/market rules defining permitted access by role.

element.name: Access Audit Trail
element.documentation: Record of access and data usage for compliance and forensics.

element.name: Authorization Server / Identity Provider (IdP)
element.documentation: Central service supporting OAuth 2.0 and OIDC.

element.name: Token Service (OAuth 2.0)
element.documentation: Issues access tokens/refresh tokens; supports delegated authorization.

element.name: OIDC Authentication Service
element.documentation: Provides identity tokens and authentication via OpenID Connect.

element.name: Authorization Policy Service
element.documentation: Manages scope-to-role-to-permission mapping and claims.

element.name: Token Revocation Service
element.documentation: Revocation endpoint for invalidating tokens.

element.name: Key Management / JWKS Publication Service
element.documentation: Key management and JWKS rotation/publication.

element.name: Monitoring and Audit Logging Service
element.documentation: Central monitoring and audit logging; supports anomaly detection.

element.name: Resource Server APIs
element.documentation: APIs enforcing scopes and claims; serve operational constraints and related information.

element.name: Protected Resource API Interface
element.documentation: API surface protected by OAuth2 access tokens.

element.name: Public Client Application
element.documentation: E.g., browser/mobile app; requires PKCE and secure token storage.

element.name: Confidential Client Application
element.documentation: E.g., server-side app/service with client secret or private key.

element.name: Operational Constraints Dataset
element.documentation: Application-level representation of operational constraints data.

element.name: Participant Identity Records
element.documentation: Registered identities of organizations/actors.

element.name: TLS-Protected Network
element.documentation: All communications protected with TLS.

element.name: Authorization Server Runtime
element.documentation: Compute/runtime environment hosting IdP services.

element.name: API Runtime
element.documentation: Compute/runtime environment hosting Resource Server APIs.

element.name: Identity Provider Platform
element.documentation: IdP/AS software supporting OAuth2/OIDC.

element.name: API Gateway / API Platform
element.documentation: Fronts APIs; can enforce authn/z, rate limits, and logging.

element.name: Hardware Security Module (HSM) Service
element.documentation: Secure key storage.

element.name: Logging and Monitoring Platform
element.documentation: Central observability for monitoring, audit logging, anomaly detection.

element.name: JWKS Key Material
element.documentation: Deployed key artifacts used to sign/validate tokens.

element.name: High Availability / Failover Service
element.documentation: Replication and failover for Authorization Server runtime.

element.name: Implement OAuth 2.0 + OIDC Standard
element.documentation: Adopt and roll out OAuth2/OIDC across platforms and participants.

element.name: OAuth2/OIDC Integration for Resource Server APIs
Key Management and JWKS Rotation Procedures
Target State: Standardized IAM across flexibility market
No standardized secure and auditable IAM mechanism
element.documentation: Current inconsistent implementations across participants.

view.name: ADR.29 — OAuth 2.0 + OpenID Connect for Identification, Authentication, Authorization — Overview
